# System status

<!-- GENERATED by scripts/refresh-docs.mjs — hand edits are destroyed. -->

> **Scope, stated plainly.** This page reports whether an env key is **present in the
> environment this script ran in** — your local machine. It is **not** a report on
> production. Production env lives only in Railway and is not readable from here.
> **Key values are never read or printed — only their names.**

*Generated 2026-07-23 · local HEAD `18fde71` (2026-07-23)*

## Service configuration (local environment)

| Service | Keys | Local |
|---|---|---|
| Supabase — database | `DATABASE_URL`, `DIRECT_URL` | ✅ configured |
| Supabase — auth (server) | `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` | ✅ configured |
| Supabase — auth (client) | `VITE_SUPABASE_URL`, `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` | ✅ configured |
| Anthropic — Claude Sonnet | `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | ✅ configured |
| Google — Gemini 2.5 Flash | `GEMINI_API_KEY` | ✅ configured |
| Resend — transactional email | `RESEND_API_KEY` | ✅ configured |
| Web push — VAPID | `VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY`, `VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY` | ✅ configured |
| PostHog — product analytics | `VITE_POSTHOG_KEY` | ✅ configured |
| Google Analytics | `VITE_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID` | ✅ configured |
| YouTube API | `YOUTUBE_API_KEY` | ✅ configured |
| IP hashing secret | `IP_HASH_SECRET` | ✅ configured |

⬜ absent locally is often **fine** — several of these are production-only. It is a
signal to check Railway, not a defect.

## Verifying production

The single check that matters, because a health endpoint returning 200 **cannot** detect a
frozen deploy — the previous image keeps serving happily:

```bash
curl -s https://app.thefantasticleagues.com/api/health | jq .version
git rev-parse origin/main       # these two must match
```

`.github/workflows/verify-deploy.yml` automates this and emails on mismatch (PR #405).
Prod has frozen twice on migration failures (`P3009`) — 21 hours in May, **8 days** in June.

---

## FUTURE: real health checks

Dormant until there are paying users. Configuration presence is not health — a key can be
present and the service still down, over quota, or rate-limited.

```js
// Per-service liveness. Wire in when uptime becomes someone else's problem, not just yours.
//
// const CHECKS = [
//   { name: 'database',  ping: () => prisma.$queryRaw`SELECT 1` },
//   { name: 'supabase',  ping: () => fetch(`${SUPABASE_URL}/auth/v1/health`) },
//   { name: 'anthropic', ping: () => fetch('https://api.anthropic.com/v1/models', { headers }) },
//   { name: 'resend',    ping: () => fetch('https://api.resend.com/domains', { headers }) },
// ];
//
// For each: status (up/down/degraded), latency p50/p95, last-success timestamp,
// 30-day uptime %, and quota headroom where the provider exposes it.
//
// Cheapest useful version first: log every outbound call's status + duration, then
// aggregate. That also closes todo 299 (no ingestion-job run tracking or alerting),
// which is a live P1 — a sync can fail silently today.
```
